note: Merged from chars-*.json. 'who' is what an agent SAW on a rendered contact sheet.
reliability: SEARCH INDEX, NOT TRUTH. An adversarial verify pass re-read 41 sampled claims across this catalog and the icon catalog and found 9 wrong (~22%). Every error was DESCRIPTIVE (a colour, 'cluster' vs 'single chunk', an instrument misnamed) — zero were wrong coordinates. So: trust sheet+charCell enough to go look; never ship a description (colour, gear, silhouette) without reading the contact sheet yourself. Render one with: node tools/contact-sheet.mjs <sheet> --mode chars --scale 8 --char c,r

entries.who: Scarecrow: a stitched tan burlap-sack head with dark carved eye slits and a jagged grin, tilted to one side; a thin brown wooden-frame body with bright orange/yellow STRAW bursting out at the neck collar, the waist, the cuffs and the ankles; long spindly arms ending in splayed straw claw-fingers; dark purple rag shadows behind the straw. Front-facing only.
entries.mobIdea: Spooky Scarecrow — field/graveyard ambusher that stands motionless until you walk past (only a front pose exists, so give it a static billboard + a rise animation)

warnings.warning: nightmare_run_1.png — index.json guesses layout 'single+char8'. The char8 reading is WRONG: the contact sheet nightmare_run_1-chars.png slices every horse in half across char-cell boundaries. The coherent reading is SINGLE: 192x192 = 3 cols x 4 rows of 64x48 frames (one character, wide frames because of the flame trail). Use the single reading.

BAKED DROP SHADOWS: lion_1, lioness_1, tiger_1, unicorn1, sasquatch_1, cerberus_1 and nightmare_run_1 all have a dark blue-gray oval shadow painted INTO the sprite. Our renderer already casts real sprite shadows — these will double up. Erase/alpha-key the oval in the extraction pipeline or accept a visible dark disc under the billboard.

horses.png — the BOTTOM char row (0,1 / 1,1 / 2,1 / 3,1) is NOT four extra breeds. It is the SAME four coat colours in a HEAD-DOWN GRAZING pose (verified by zooming frame (0,128) at 18x: a palomino with its muzzle on the ground). Directions are still present. Use the top row for walking mobs, the bottom row for idle/grazing ambience.
animals1/2/3 + horses are 32x32 quadruped cells and DO decompose cleanly as char8 (4x2 chars, each 3 cols x 4 rows). No off-grid halves found on any of the four.

animals2 char (0,1) and (1,1) read as 'doubled/overlapping cows' at contact-sheet scale. Zoomed to 18x on raw frame (0,128..) they are clean single cows/bulls — a scale artefact, NOT a grid bug. They are simply darker-toned recolours of the (0,0)/(1,0) cow and bull.

Quadruped row order on the 32x32 sheets is the standard 4-row walk grid, but the artist drew the 'down' row as a near-profile view too, so all four rows look side-on. Direction picking will need eyeballing per animal rather than assuming down=front.
chameleon / platypus / badger / bushbaby / butterflyA / butterflyB / gorilla_1 all share the 42x36 frame (126x144 sheet) = single character, 3x4. All four rows are on-grid and legible.
No sheet in this group is a humanoid walk sheet except the two werewolves and sasquatch — heightM for the quadrupeds is estimated from body proportions against the existing wolf=1.2 convention.

No off-grid or garbled sheets found in this group — all 20 decomposed cleanly into a 3x3-visible walk grid across 4 direction rows (down/left/right/up), consistent with index.json's 'single' layout. NOTE: the contact-sheet filenames all carry the '-chars' suffix, which is misleading; NONE of these is an 8-character char8 sheet. Two per-sheet observations worth flagging: (1) gryphon_baby_1's sprite fills only a small fraction of its 52x53 cell (a deliberately tiny cub), so a naive full-cell billboard will float it; trim to content. (2) beholder_a_1's up-facing row is an intentionally featureless gray dome (the back of its head) rather than damaged art. (3) beholder_a has short blunt nubs around the crown, NOT eyestalks; only beholder_b has true eyestalks with eyeballs. (4) centaur_a/b/c and both trolls are drawn UNARMED — any weapon must come from FX/projectiles, not the sprite.

NAMING ARTIFACT (not a content problem): every contact sheet in this group is named '<name>-chars.png' even for the single-character sheets. index.json is the source of truth for layout: only angel.png and angel_wingless.png are char8 (456x288, 38x36 frames); everything else is a single character.

NO off-grid halves found in this group. All 21 sheets decomposed cleanly into their stated grids: the two char8 sheets read as 4x2 characters each with a coherent 3-col x 4-row (down/left/right/up) walk grid, and every 'single' sheet read as exactly one 3x4 walk grid. Nothing resembling the wizard.png / elemental.png bottom-half garbage was observed.

angel.png 1,1 vs angel_wingless.png 1,1: in the WINGED sheet the dark teal shape is a pair of slate-blue feathered wings; in the WINGLESS sheet the same character keeps a tall dark-teal HOODED CLOAK/MANTLE behind the head (the up-view is a plain teal cylinder, not feathers). Do not describe the wingless one as winged.
demonkingb_full_mowings_1.png — 'mowings' is a typo in the source filename for 'nowings'. Content is the wingless variant of demonkingb_full_wings_1.png.

dragon_big_* (107x92) and wyvern_single_* (112x80) share head/wing art. The distinguishing feature I verified visually: the big dragons show four limbs (forelegs under the neck in the side views), the wyverns show only hind legs with the wings serving as forelimbs. If a pipeline ever needs to be certain, re-inspect the left/right rows at >=6x zoom.

dragon_red_reg / dragon_green_reg (52x53) are drawn in a much flatter, simpler 3-tone style than every other sheet in this group and are BIPEDAL, pot-bellied drakes — they are NOT small versions of dragon_big_*. Mixing them in the same scene will look stylistically off.

boss_giant_1.png is drawn in a restricted sepia/tan palette (browns, creams, one dark outline) with no other hues. It will read as 'monochrome' next to the full-color bosses; consider a tint pass or accept it as a stone/earth giant.
boss_statue_west_1.png is likewise fully monochrome white/gray (marble) by design, and boss_statue_east_1.png is fully monochrome gold. Both are statues, so this is intentional.

Frame boxes are large relative to the drawn sprite on several 107x92 sheets (boss_giant especially — the figure fills maybe half the frame height). Billboard heightM below assumes the sprite is alpha-trimmed first, as the existing pipeline does.

slimevariants_1: layout guess 'char8' is HALF WRONG. Only the TOP row of character blocks (0,0 / 1,0 / 2,0 / 3,0) contains art. The whole bottom row (0,1 / 1,1 / 2,1 / 3,1) is empty transparent cells. Treat as a 4-character sheet. Also, within each character the 4 'direction' rows are IDENTICAL — the 3 columns are a squash/bounce cycle, not a walk cycle. Usable directly (all facings look the same, which is correct for a blob).
slime_big_single / slime_king_single: 3x4 grid exists, but all 4 direction rows are identical; the 3 columns are a squash/stretch bounce. Non-directional by design.

witch1_cauldron_1 / witch2_cauldron_1: NOT a walk grid. The source is 96x192 = 3 cols x 4 rows at 32x48, but ONLY THE MIDDLE COLUMN has pixels; columns 0 and 2 are fully empty (the contact sheet paints them as solid orange/green empty-cell markers). What exists is a 4-frame front-facing 'stirring the cauldron' idle animation. Use as an animated prop/NPC, not as a walking character.

lantphant_rm_1: layout guess 'char8' is only partly right. Character blocks 0,0 and 0,1 are full 3-column strips. Blocks 1,0 / 1,1 / 2,0 / 2,1 / 3,0 / 3,1 have ONLY their middle column populated (single-column animation strips); cols 0 and 2 are empty. Also every 'direction' row of 0,0 / 0,1 looks front-facing — this is an idle/flicker animation set, not a 4-direction walk grid.

beast_hero_*_emote (all 5): layout guess 'single' (3x4 walk grid) is WRONG. All 12 frames are FRONT-FACING poses of the same hero as the matching beast_hero_N sheet, varying arm position / facial expression. These are emote/portrait pose sheets, not walk grids. Do not map them to down/left/right/up.

chocobos_saddle_1 / mondinos_1 / mondinos_saddle_1: 8 character blocks = 4 color morphs, each duplicated. The top-row block and its bottom-row twin are the same creature; the bottom-row copies are drawn WITH a baked-in elliptical ground shadow, the top-row ones WITHOUT. Prefer the top row (the engine draws its own shadows).

beast_tribe_5 char 1,1: a fishfolk in a hood rendered almost entirely in flat desaturated gray with almost no interior detail. Could be a shadow/silhouette palette variant or an unfinished cell — treat as low confidence.
warelementals_1 / warelementals_f_1: both decompose cleanly into 8 x (3 cols x 4 rows). No off-grid junk half (unlike wizard.png/elemental.png). Rows read down / left / right / up in the usual order.

shark_full_blue_1 / shark_full_brown_1: valid 3x4 grid, but the 'down' row is a head-on top-down view and the 'up' row is a tail-on top-down view. This is a top-down swim sheet; billboarding it in a first-person 3D world will look wrong from most angles unless you only ever use the left/right profile rows.
pirates_100.png: the ENTIRE bottom character row (cells 0,1..3,1) is fully transparent — 4 usable characters, not 8. Verified by alpha-count = 0 over each 78x144 block.
shamans_100.png: same — bottom character row (0,1..3,1) is fully transparent. 4 usable characters.

slicer_rmsheet.png: the 'char8' layout guess is wrong in meaning. 384x512 at 32x64 does decompose into 8 blocks of 3x4, but every block is the SAME creature: they are animation sets (walk / run / attack / lunge), and blocks 2,0, 3,0 and 3,1 contain slash-FX and tumbling poses that are NOT down/left/right/up rows. Only 0,0 and 1,0 are usable as a directional walk grid. Classify as a single character.

ratmen.png: not 8 characters. It is 4 fur colors duplicated — the bottom character row is pixel-identical to the top except for a baked-in elliptical drop shadow under the feet (339 differing px per pair). Use the TOP row; a baked shadow would double up with the engine's real entity shadows.

executioner_axe_1.png: the axe head runs flush to the LEFT EDGE of its 26px cell in the down-facing middle frame (11 opaque rows at x=0). Any alpha-trim / rect-window extraction must grab the exact 26x36 cell or it will weld the neighbouring frame's pixels onto the sprite (the known outside.png trap).

Golden Spikey Knight Sprite Sheet.png: 94x100 frames — a completely different pixel density and art style from the 26x36 Time Fantasy sheets. Its 3x4 walk grid is valid, but it will not visually match TF mobs standing next to it. Boss-only use.

goblinkinga_1.png / goblinkingb_1.png: 52x53 cells but the sprite fills only the middle of the cell; do not infer a large creature from the frame size. They are goblino-sized (~1.5 m) with a crown and cape.
gnomea_reg / gnomeb_reg: sprite occupies only the lower ~55% of the 26x36 cell (a genuinely short character), not a misalignment.

cultists_masks_1.png packs its 8 cells as 4 palettes x 2 trim variants, while cultists_1 and cultists_b_1 pack theirs as 4 designs x 2 palettes. Do not assume the same cell means the same variant across the three cultist sheets.
No sheet in this group had a garbled / off-grid half of the wizard.png kind. bandits, cultists x3, darkelf x2, goblinos, paladin, ratmen x2 all decompose cleanly into 8 x (3 cols x 4 rows).

spookyscarecrow_1.png (84x144) does NOT decompose into a 3x4 walk grid at 28x36. Alpha row-scan: content bands at y 22-33 (head/hat crown only), y 39-69 (full body), y 75-107 (full body, slightly different pose), and y 108-143 is COMPLETELY EMPTY. So it is a 3-column x ~3-row front-facing sequence (reads like an emergence/rise: head-only -> full body -> full body), with a blank 4th row. There is no left/right/up view. Treat as a single front-facing sprite (+3-frame idle); it is NOT usable as a 4-direction walker.

mzombies_ground_a_1.png is rendered as char8 but is NOT eight characters. Six of the eight blocks are ground/emergence FX (dirt mound swelling; zombie torso clawing out of a dirt ring; open grave hole with a bony hand reaching up). Only blocks 2,0 and 2,1 are true 3x4 four-direction walk grids (the risen maroon-shirt zombie and the risen white-shirt zombie). Do not map the other six as characters.

skeletonarmy_down.png is rendered as char8 and does decompose into 8 blocks of 3x4, but every frame is a PRONE/collapsed corpse (top-down sprawled bones), not a directional walk. All 12 frames per block are near-identical. Use as a dormant/corpse state paired with skeletonarmy.png, not as a walker.

vamps_8_1.png bottom row (cy=1) is a palette variant, not four new characters: same four vampires with warm living skin instead of chalk-pale. Same for mzombies_1.png bottom row (bloodied versions of the same four zombies).
reaper_blade_1.png uses a WIDER 32x36 frame (96x144 sheet) than reaper_1.png's 26x36 (78x144) — do not reuse the same frame width for both.

No off-grid/garbled halves were found in lich.png, skeletonarmy.png, mzombies_1.png or vamps_8_1.png — all four are clean 8-character sheets with 3x4 walk grids in BOTH halves (unlike the historical wizard.png/elemental.png trap).
bigskullA/B/C/D_1.png are single-character 32x48 sheets with clean 3x4 grids; the pairs are (A jawless bone / B jawed bone) and (C jawless flaming / D jawed flaming).
